name: Úbeda and El Condado Loop
name_original: Santisteban del Puerto – vuelta por Úbeda y Castellar
description: This difficult 62.0-mile (99.7 km) road cycling route connects Santisteban del Puerto, Úbeda, and Castellar, with 4778 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santisteban Del Puerto are situated in the foothills of Sierra Morena, offering a varied and dynamic terrain. The landscape features a mix of climbs and descents, characteristic of the region's "contrasts of relief." Extensive olive groves and natural meadows provide scenic backdrops for rides. This area in Jaén, Spain, is defined by its mountainous sections and Mediterranean forest. Best road cycling routes around Santisteban Del Puerto The most popular road cycling route is Úbeda and El Condado Loop, a 61.9 miles (99.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mogón Natural Pool – Castillo de Sabiote loop from Castellar, a difficult 68.9 miles (110.9 km) path. This route offers views of natural pools and historic castles. Local road cyclists also love the Beas de Segura - La Teja loop from Castellar, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra Morena foothills, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Santisteban Del Puerto is defined by Sierra Morena foothills, extensive olive groves, and natural meadows. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santisteban Del Puerto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Santisteban del Puerto?
A: The region, nestled in the foothills of Sierra Morena, offers a varied and dynamic terrain. You'll encounter a mix of climbs and descents, characteristic of the area's 'contrasts of relief.' Expect scenic rides through extensive olive groves and natural meadows, with mountainous sections providing a beautiful backdrop.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santisteban del Puerto?
A: There are nearly 20 road cycling routes around Santisteban del Puerto documented on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for different abilities. While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy route and several moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beas de Segura - La Teja loop from Castellar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.3-mile route that takes about 3 hours 12 minutes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging terrain. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, with 10 out of the 19 routes classified as difficult. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009332387/ubeda-and-el-condado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and El Condado Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 61.9-mile route with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Santisteban del Puerto?
A: You can encounter a variety of natural and historical points of interest. Some routes pass near unique sites like the dinosaur footprints, a Natural Monument just 1 km from town. You might also find yourself near the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there any scenic road cycling routes that offer great views?
A: Many routes in the area are highly scenic, winding through extensive olive groves and natural meadows with the Sierra Morena foothills as a backdrop. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mogón Natural Pool – Castillo de Sabiote loop from Castellar&lt;/a&gt; offers views of natural pools and historic castles, providing diverse scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009332387/ubeda-and-el-condado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and El Condado Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navas de San Juan – Dinosaur Tracks loop from Castellar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santisteban del Puerto?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes it suitable for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer. The varied landscape provides beautiful views throughout the seasons.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the area boasts beautiful natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mogón Natural Pool – Castillo de Sabiote loop from Castellar&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights natural pools. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cimbarra Mill&lt;/a&gt; are notable attractions in the vicinity that can be incorporated into or viewed from cycling routes.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Sierra Morena foothills directly from Santisteban del Puerto?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guaria – Chiclana de Segura loop from Santisteban del Puerto&lt;/a&gt; directly explore the Sierra Morena foothills, offering the characteristic varied terrain and Mediterranean forest landscapes of the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Santisteban del Puerto?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse and dynamic terrain, which offers a good mix of climbs and descents. The extensive olive groves and natural meadows provide picturesque scenery, making for engaging and visually rewarding rides. Over 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore this area, highlighting its appeal for varied cycling experiences.
tips: Small town dating from the middle of the II millennium BC. C. The Church of San Juan Bautista is located in the center of town and is dedicated to the patron saint of the town. The production of olive oil and the running of the bulls play a central role in the life and festivals of the town.

The A-301 highway crosses the Guadalquivir river at its mouth in the Giribaile reservoir. During the rainy season, the river and reservoir teem with life, but can be dry in summer. From the highway you can see the Ariza Bridge, a 15th century masonry bridge over the old road that connected Úbeda with Arquillos. The slopes are dotted with old watchtowers.

This monumental bullring was inaugurated in 1857 where there was an old convent known as San Nicasio, founded at the beginning of the 15th century. 
The inauguration date was that year of 1857 in which the bullfighter "Lagartijo" took the alternative, the same one who later inaugurated the Baeza bullring.

Úbeda is an important city, and one of the most glorious and stately in Andalusia. The oldest parts include the Tower and the Cava Walls. It is believed that it settled in Roman times. Its stone walls were built in 852 and continually fought and changed hands over the centuries between Muslim and Christian rulers.

A small town located at the foot of the Sierra Morena mountains. The town is surrounded by olive groves typical of the area that support the production of olive oil. Like many settlements in the area, Castellar dates back to prehistoric times, with historical finds from Iberian and Roman times.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Santisteban del Puerto – return via Úbeda and Castellar route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, classified as a 'Hard road ride'. It requires a very good level of fitness due to its length and significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is estimated to take approximately 4 hours and 57 minutes to complete, assuming a steady pace for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for road cycling. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and difficult to ride, adding an extra layer of challenge.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through several interesting locations. Highlights include the town of Navas de San Juan, a View of the Guadalquivir River and Giribaile Reservoir, and the historic Plaza de Toros de Úbeda. Don't miss the Views from the Alcázar District in Úbeda, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the charming Castellar Town Square and Fountain.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Santisteban del Puerto, Úbeda, and Castellar are towns with public parking options. It's advisable to check local regulations for parking availability suitable for cyclists, especially near the presumed starting point in Santisteban del Puerto.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the climate in Andalusia, the best times to cycle this route are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this public road cycling route through the Jaén province of Andalusia.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists due to traffic and the demanding nature of the ride. If you plan to bring a dog, consider alternative, dedicated walking or less trafficked routes.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it briefly intersects with sections of local paths such as Camino del patronato, Cañada Real El Paso, and Cordel del Puerto de los Aceiteros.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns including Santisteban del Puerto, Úbeda, and Castellar. These towns offer various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation where you can refuel and rest.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essential cycling gear, including a repair kit, sufficient water and nutrition, appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions, and sun protection. Due to potentially unpaved segments, consider tires suitable for varied surfaces.

waypoints.name: View of the Guadalquivir River and Giribaile Reservoir
